data.problem_definition: Many users require a convenient, accessible, and reliable tool for performing mathematical calculations directly within their web browser without needing to download or install dedicated software. Existing solutions might be overly complex, lack essential features, or have poor user experience.
user_personas: Student Sarah: A high school student who needs to quickly solve homework problems (algebra, geometry) and check answers. She values ease of use, accuracy, and accessibility from any device.

Office Worker Oliver: An office professional who needs to perform quick calculations for budgets, reports, or data analysis. He values efficiency, a clean interface, and the ability to recall previous calculations.

Engineer Emily: An engineering student or professional who occasionally needs scientific functions (trigonometry, logarithms, exponents) for complex problem-solving. She values precision and a comprehensive set of functions.
use_cases: Basic Calculation: User needs to calculate "15 * 3 + 7".
Scientific Calculation: User needs to calculate "sin(90) + log(100)".
Budgeting: User needs to sum several expenses and then subtract a discount.
Error Handling: User attempts to divide by zero and expects an error message.
History Recall: User wants to review a previous calculation result.
user_flow: User opens the web calculator application.

in_scope: Scientific functions (sin, cos, tan, log, ln, sqrt, power, pi, e)
Responsive design for various screen sizes
Error handling for invalid mathematical operations (e.g., division by zero)
out_of_scope: Offline functionality (e.g., Progressive Web App features)
Advanced statistical functions (e.g., mean, median, standard deviation)
User accounts or saving calculations to a server
Complex financial calculations (e.g., loan amortization, interest rates)
Integration with other applications or services
